     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Causal Edges
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Causal edge creation (memory_causal_link)
     5	// Causal relationship graph for memory lineage
     6	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     7	import { clearDegreeCacheForDb } from '../search/graph-search-fn.js';
     8	import { clearGraphSignalsCache } from '../graph/graph-signals.js';
     9	import { detectContradictions } from '../graph/contradiction-detection.js';
    10	import { ensureTemporalColumns } from '../graph/temporal-edges.js';
    11	import { isTemporalEdgesEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
    12	import { runInTransaction } from './transaction-manager.js';
    13	
    14	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	   1. CONSTANTS
    16	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    17	
    18	const RELATION_TYPES = Object.freeze({
    19	  CAUSED: 'caused',
    20	  ENABLED: 'enabled',
    21	  SUPERSEDES: 'supersedes',
    22	  CONTRADICTS: 'contradicts',
    23	  DERIVED_FROM: 'derived_from',
    24	  SUPPORTS: 'supports',
    25	} as const);
    26	
    27	type RelationType = typeof RELATION_TYPES[keyof typeof RELATION_TYPES];
    28	
    29	/**
    30	 * C138: Relation weight multipliers applied during traversal scoring.
    31	 * Higher values amplify the propagated strength; values < 1.0 dampen it.
    32	 */
    33	const RELATION_WEIGHTS: Record<string, number> = {
    34	  supersedes:   1.5,  // Strongest signal — new info replaces old
    35	  caused:       1.3,  // Strong causal link
    36	  enabled:      1.1,  // Weak causal link
    37	  supports:     1.0,  // Neutral / default
    38	  derived_from: 1.0,  // Neutral / default
    39	  contradicts:  0.8,  // Dampened — conflicting signals lower confidence
    40	};
    41	
    42	const DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH = 3;
    43	const MAX_EDGES_LIMIT = 100;
    44	
    45	// Edge bounds for the lightweight runtime path (NFR-R01, SC-005)
    46	const MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE = 20;
    47	const MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH = 0.5;
    48	const MAX_STRENGTH_INCREASE_PER_CYCLE = 0.05;
    49	const STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 90;
    50	const DECAY_STRENGTH_AMOUNT = 0.1;
    51	const DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS = 30;
    52	
    53	function isFiniteNumber(value: unknown): value is number {
    54	  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value);
    55	}
    56	
    57	function clampStrength(strength: number): number | null {
    58	  if (!Number.isFinite(strength)) return null;
    59	  return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, strength));
    60	}
    61	
    62	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	   2. INTERFACES
    64	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    65	
    66	interface CausalEdge {
    67	  id: number;
    68	  source_id: string;
    69	  target_id: string;
    70	  relation: RelationType;
    71	  strength: number;
    72	  evidence: string | null;
    73	  extracted_at: string;
    74	  created_by: string;
    75	  last_accessed: string | null;
    76	}
    77	
    78	type EdgeQueryResult = CausalEdge[] & {
    79	  truncated: boolean;
    80	  limit: number;
    81	};
    82	
    83	interface WeightHistoryEntry {
    84	  id: number;
    85	  edge_id: number;
    86	  old_strength: number;
    87	  new_strength: number;
    88	  changed_by: string;
    89	  changed_at: string;
    90	  reason: string | null;
    91	}
    92	
    93	interface GraphStats {
    94	  totalEdges: number;
    95	  byRelation: Record<string, number>;
    96	  avgStrength: number;
    97	  uniqueSources: number;
    98	  uniqueTargets: number;
    99	}
   100	
   101	interface CausalChainNode {
   102	  id: string;
   103	  edgeId?: number;          // T202: causal_edges.id for unlink workflow
   104	  depth: number;
   105	  relation: RelationType;
   106	  strength: number;
   107	  children: CausalChainNode[];
   108	  truncated?: boolean;
   109	  truncationLimit?: number | null;
   110	}
   111	
   112	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113	   3. MODULE STATE
   114	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   115	
   116	let db: Database.Database | null = null;
   117	
   118	function invalidateDegreeCache(): void {
   119	  try {
   120	    // H1 FIX: Use db-specific cache invalidation instead of the no-op global version
   121	    if (db) {
   122	      clearDegreeCacheForDb(db);
   123	    }
   124	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
   125	    // Cache invalidation is best-effort; never block edge mutations
   126	  }
   127	  try {
   128	    clearGraphSignalsCache();
   129	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
   130	    // Graph signals cache invalidation is best-effort
   131	  }
   132	}
   133	
   134	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   135	   4. INITIALIZATION
   136	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   137	
   138	function init(database: Database.Database): void {
   139	  db = database;
   140	
   141	  // Defensive traversal indexes — the canonical creation lives in
   142	  // Vector-index-impl.ts migration v8 (CHK-062).  These IF NOT EXISTS guards
   143	  // Ensure the indexes are present even when the module is initialised outside
   144	  // The normal migration path (e.g. tests, standalone scripts).
   145	  try {
   146	    database.exec('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_causal_source ON causal_edges(source_id)');
   147	    database.exec('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_causal_target ON causal_edges(target_id)');
   148	    ensureTemporalColumns(database);
   149	  } catch (_e: unknown) {
   150	    // Best-effort: table may not exist yet during early startup sequencing
   151	  }
   152	}
   153	
   154	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   155	   5. EDGE OPERATIONS
   156	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   157	
   158	function insertEdge(
   159	  sourceId: string,
   160	  targetId: string,
   161	  relation: RelationType,
   162	  strength: number = 1.0,
   163	  evidence: string | null = null,
   164	  shouldInvalidateCache: boolean = true,
   165	  createdBy: string = 'manual',
   166	): number | null {
   167	  if (!db) {
   168	    console.warn('[causal-edges] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.');
   169	    return null;
   170	  }
   171	  const database = db;
   172	
   173	  // NFR-R01: Auto edges capped at MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH
   174	  const effectiveStrength = createdBy === 'auto'
   175	    ? Math.min(strength, MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH)
   176	    : strength;
   177	
   178	  // Prevent self-loops
   179	  if (sourceId === targetId) {
   180	    return null;
   181	  }
   182	
   183	  // Fix #26 (FK check) deferred — test environments use synthetic IDs not in memory_index.
   184	  // Implementing FK validation would require seeding memory_index in 20+ causal edge tests.
   185	
   186	  // NFR-R01: Edge bounds — reject if node already has MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE auto edges
   187	  if (createdBy === 'auto') {
   188	    const edgeCount = countEdgesForNode(sourceId);
   189	    if (edgeCount >= MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE) {
   190	      console.warn(`[causal-edges] Edge bounds: node ${sourceId} has ${edgeCount} edges (max ${MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE}), rejecting auto edge`);
   191	      return null;
   192	    }
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  try {
   196	    const clampedStrength = clampStrength(effectiveStrength);
   197	    if (clampedStrength === null) {
   198	      console.warn('[causal-edges] insertEdge rejected non-finite strength');
   199	      return null;
   200	    }
   201	
   202	    // Wrap SELECT + UPSERT + logWeightChange in a transaction for atomicity
   203	    const rowId = database.transaction(() => {
   204	      if (isTemporalEdgesEnabled()) {
   205	        detectContradictions(
   206	          database,
   207	          Number.parseInt(sourceId, 10),
   208	          Number.parseInt(targetId, 10),
   209	          relation,
   210	        );
   211	      }
   212	
   213	      // Check if edge exists (for weight_history logging on conflict update).
   214	      // This SELECT is intentional: we need the old strength to decide whether
   215	      // To write a weight_history row after the upsert. The subsequent INSERT
   216	      // Uses last_insert_rowid() to avoid a second post-upsert SELECT.
   217	      const existing = (database.prepare(`
   218	        SELECT id, strength FROM causal_edges
   219	        WHERE source_id = ? AND target_id = ? AND relation = ?
   220	      `) as Database.Statement).get(sourceId, targetId, relation) as { id: number; strength: number } | undefined;
   221	
   222	      (database.prepare(`
   223	        INSERT INTO causal_edges (source_id, target_id, relation, strength, evidence, created_by)
   224	        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   225	        ON CONFLICT(source_id, target_id, relation) DO UPDATE SET
   226	          strength = excluded.strength,
   227	          evidence = COALESCE(excluded.evidence, causal_edges.evidence)
   228	      `) as Database.Statement).run(sourceId, targetId, relation, clampedStrength, evidence, createdBy);
   229	
   230	      const row = (database.prepare(`
   231	        SELECT id FROM causal_edges WHERE source_id = ? AND target_id = ? AND relation = ?
   232	      `) as Database.Statement).get(sourceId, targetId, relation) as { id: number } | undefined;
   233	      const rowId = row ? row.id : 0;
   234	
   235	      // T001d: Log weight change on conflict update
   236	      if (existing && rowId && isFiniteNumber(existing.strength) && existing.strength !== clampedStrength) {
   237	        logWeightChange(rowId, existing.strength, clampedStrength, createdBy, 'insert-upsert');
   238	      }
   239	
   240	      return rowId;
   241	    })();
   242	
   243	    if (shouldInvalidateCache) {
   244	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   245	    }
   246	
   247	    return rowId || null;
   248	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   249	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   250	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] insertEdge error: ${msg}`);
   251	    if (/SQLITE_BUSY|SQLITE_LOCKED|database is locked/i.test(msg)) {
   252	      throw error;
   253	    }
   254	    return null;
   255	  }
   256	}
   257	
   258	function insertEdgesBatch(
   259	  edges: Array<{
   260	    sourceId: string;
   520	      if (clampedStrength === null) {
   521	        console.warn('[causal-edges] updateEdge rejected non-finite strength');
   522	        return false;
   523	      }
   524	      nextStrength = clampedStrength;
   525	      parts.push('strength = ?');
   526	      params.push(clampedStrength);
   527	    }
   528	    if (updates.evidence !== undefined) {
   529	      parts.push('evidence = ?');
   530	      params.push(updates.evidence);
   531	    }
   532	
   533	    if (parts.length === 0) return false;
   534	
   535	    params.push(edgeId);
   536	
   537	    // Wrap SELECT + UPDATE + logWeightChange in a transaction for atomicity
   538	    const changed = database.transaction(() => {
   539	      // T001d: Capture old strength for weight_history logging
   540	      let oldStrength: number | undefined;
   541	      if (updates.strength !== undefined) {
   542	        const existing = (database.prepare(
   543	          'SELECT strength FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ?'
   544	        ) as Database.Statement).get(edgeId) as { strength: number } | undefined;
   545	        oldStrength = existing?.strength;
   546	      }
   547	
   548	      // String interpolation constructs IN(?,?,?) placeholder list only —
   549	      // All user values are parameterized. Accepted exception per audit H-08.
   550	      const result = (database.prepare(
   551	        `UPDATE causal_edges SET ${parts.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`
   552	      ) as Database.Statement).run(...params);
   553	
   554	      const changed = (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
   555	
   556	      // T001d: Log weight change to weight_history
   557	      if (changed && nextStrength !== undefined && isFiniteNumber(oldStrength)) {
   558	        if (oldStrength !== nextStrength) {
   559	          logWeightChange(edgeId, oldStrength, nextStrength, changedBy, reason);
   560	        }
   561	      }
   562	
   563	      return changed;
   564	    })();
   565	
   566	    if (changed) {
   567	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   568	    }
   569	
   570	    return changed;
   571	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   572	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   573	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] updateEdge error: ${msg}`);
   574	    return false;
   575	  }
   576	}
   577	
   578	function deleteEdge(edgeId: number): boolean {
   579	  if (!db) return false;
   580	
   581	  try {
   582	    const result = (db.prepare(
   583	      'DELETE FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ?'
   584	    ) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId);
   585	    if ((result as { changes: number }).changes > 0) {
   586	      invalidateDegreeCache();
   587	    }
   588	    return (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
   589	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   590	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   591	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] deleteEdge error: ${msg}`);
   592	    return false;
   593	  }
   594	}
   595	
   596	// Let errors propagate so callers inside transactions see failures.
   597	// Previously errors were caught and swallowed, which hid edge-cleanup failures
   598	// From transactional callers (e.g., memory-bulk-delete, memory-crud-delete).
   599	function deleteEdgesForMemory(memoryId: string): number {
   600	  if (!db) return 0;
   601	
   602	  const result = (db.prepare(`
   603	    DELETE FROM causal_edges
   604	    WHERE source_id = ? OR target_id = ?
   605	  `) as Database.Statement).run(memoryId, memoryId);
   606	  if ((result as { changes: number }).changes > 0) {
   607	    invalidateDegreeCache();
   608	  }
   609	  return (result as { changes: number }).changes;
   610	}
   611	
   612	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   613	   8. STATS & QUERIES
   614	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   615	
   616	function getGraphStats(): GraphStats {
   617	  if (!db) {
   618	    return { totalEdges: 0, byRelation: {}, avgStrength: 0, uniqueSources: 0, uniqueTargets: 0 };
   619	  }
   620	
   621	  try {
   622	    const total = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   623	    const byRelation = (db.prepare('SELECT relation, COUNT(*) as count FROM causal_edges GROUP BY relation') as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{ relation: string; count: number }>;
   624	    const avgStrength = (db.prepare('SELECT AVG(strength) as avg FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { avg: number | null };
   625	    const sources = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT source_id) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   626	    const targets = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT target_id) as count FROM causal_edges') as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
   627	
   628	    const relationMap: Record<string, number> = {};
   629	    for (const row of byRelation) {
   630	      relationMap[row.relation] = row.count;
   631	    }
   632	
   633	    return {
   634	      totalEdges: total.count,
   635	      byRelation: relationMap,
   636	      avgStrength: Math.round((avgStrength.avg || 0) * 100) / 100,
   637	      uniqueSources: sources.count,
   638	      uniqueTargets: targets.count,
   639	    };
   640	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   641	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   642	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getGraphStats error: ${msg}`);
   643	    return { totalEdges: 0, byRelation: {}, avgStrength: 0, uniqueSources: 0, uniqueTargets: 0 };
   644	  }
   645	}
   646	
   647	function findOrphanedEdges(): CausalEdge[] {
   648	  if (!db) return [];
   649	
   650	  try {
   651	    return (db.prepare(`
   652	      SELECT ce.* FROM causal_edges ce
   653	      WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index m WHERE CAST(m.id AS TEXT) = ce.source_id)
   654	        OR NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM memory_index m WHERE CAST(m.id AS TEXT) = ce.target_id)
   655	    `) as Database.Statement).all() as CausalEdge[];
   656	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   657	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   658	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] findOrphanedEdges error: ${msg}`);
   659	    return [];
   660	  }
   661	}
   662	
   663	// Automated orphan edge cleanup
   664	function cleanupOrphanedEdges(): { deleted: number } {
   665	  if (!db) return { deleted: 0 };
   666	  try {
   667	    const orphaned = findOrphanedEdges();
   668	    const deleted = runInTransaction(db, () => {
   669	      let count = 0;
   670	      for (const edge of orphaned) {
   671	        if (deleteEdge(edge.id)) count++;
   672	      }
   673	      return count;
   674	    });
   675	    return { deleted };
   676	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   677	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   678	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] cleanupOrphanedEdges error: ${msg}`);
   679	    return { deleted: 0 };
   680	  }
   681	}
   682	
   683	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   684	   9. SPEC DOCUMENT CHAINS (Spec 126)
   685	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   686	
   687	/**
   688	 * Create causal relationship chain between spec folder documents.
   689	 * Links: spec -> plan (CAUSED), plan -> tasks (CAUSED), tasks -> impl-summary (CAUSED)
   690	 * Also: checklist SUPPORTS spec, decision-record SUPPORTS plan, research SUPPORTS spec
   691	 *
   692	 * @param documentIds Map of document_type -> memory_index.id for documents in the same spec folder
   693	 */
   694	function createSpecDocumentChain(documentIds: Record<string, number>): { inserted: number; failed: number } {
   695	  if (!db) return { inserted: 0, failed: 0 };
   696	
   697	  const edges: Array<{
   698	    sourceId: string;
   699	    targetId: string;
   700	    relation: RelationType;
   701	    strength?: number;
   702	    evidence?: string | null;
   703	  }> = [];
   704	
   705	  const ids = documentIds;
   706	
   707	  // Primary chain: spec -> plan -> tasks -> implementation_summary
   708	  if (ids.spec && ids.plan) {
   709	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.spec), targetId: String(ids.plan), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.9, evidence: 'Spec 126: spec -> plan chain' });
   710	  }
   711	  if (ids.plan && ids.tasks) {
   712	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.plan), targetId: String(ids.tasks), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.9, evidence: 'Spec 126: plan -> tasks chain' });
   713	  }
   714	  if (ids.tasks && ids.implementation_summary) {
   715	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.tasks), targetId: String(ids.implementation_summary), relation: RELATION_TYPES.CAUSED, strength: 0.8, evidence: 'Spec 126: tasks -> impl-summary chain' });
   716	  }
   717	
   718	  // Support relationships
   719	  if (ids.checklist && ids.spec) {
   720	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.checklist), targetId: String(ids.spec), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.7, evidence: 'Spec 126: checklist supports spec' });
   721	  }
   722	  if (ids.decision_record && ids.plan) {
   723	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.decision_record), targetId: String(ids.plan), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.8, evidence: 'Spec 126: decision-record supports plan' });
   724	  }
   725	  if (ids.research && ids.spec) {
   726	    edges.push({ sourceId: String(ids.research), targetId: String(ids.spec), relation: RELATION_TYPES.SUPPORTS, strength: 0.7, evidence: 'Spec 126: research supports spec' });
   727	  }
   728	
   729	  if (edges.length === 0) return { inserted: 0, failed: 0 };
   730	
   731	  return insertEdgesBatch(edges);
   732	}
   733	
   734	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   735	   10. WEIGHT HISTORY & AUDIT (T001d)
   736	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   737	
   738	function logWeightChange(
   739	  edgeId: number,
   740	  oldStrength: number,
   741	  newStrength: number,
   742	  changedBy: string = 'manual',
   743	  reason: string | null = null,
   744	): void {
   745	  if (!db) return;
   746	  (db.prepare(`
   747	    INSERT INTO weight_history (edge_id, old_strength, new_strength, changed_by, reason)
   748	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   749	  `) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId, oldStrength, newStrength, changedBy, reason);
   750	}
   751	
   752	function getWeightHistory(edgeId: number, limit: number = 50): WeightHistoryEntry[] {
   753	  if (!db) return [];
   754	  try {
   755	    return (db.prepare(`
   756	      SELECT * FROM weight_history
   757	      WHERE edge_id = ?
   758	      ORDER BY changed_at DESC, id DESC
   759	      LIMIT ?
   760	    `) as Database.Statement).all(edgeId, limit) as WeightHistoryEntry[];

    70	  --stdin           Read structured JSON from stdin (preferred when a caller already has curated session data)
    71	  --json <string>   Read structured JSON from an inline string (preferred when structured data is available)
    72	  --session-id <uuid>  Explicit session ID to attach to the saved memory metadata
    73	
    74	Examples:
    75	  node generate-context.js /tmp/context-data.json

    78	  echo '{"specFolder":"specs/001-feature/"}' | node generate-context.js --stdin

    80	
    81	Output:
    82	  Creates a memory file in <spec-folder>/memory/ with ANCHOR format
    83	  for indexing by the Spec Kit Memory system.
    84	
    85	Preferred save path (JSON-PRIMARY):
    86	  - ALWAYS use --stdin, --json, or a JSON temp file.
    87	  - The AI has strictly better information about its own session than any DB query can reconstruct.
    88	  - Explicit CLI targets still outrank payload specFolder values in every structured-input mode.
    89	
    90	Direct CLI target rule:
    91	  - When a spec folder is passed on the CLI, that explicit target is authoritative.
    92	  - Session learning, JSON SPEC_FOLDER fields, and auto-detect may inform logging,
    93	    but they must not reroute the save to another folder.
    94	
    95	JSON Data Format (with preflight/postflight, session/git, and tool/exchange enrichment):
    96	  {
    97	    "user_prompts": [...],
    98	    "observations": [...],
    99	    "recent_context": [...],
   100	    "toolCalls": [
   101	      { "tool": "Read", "inputSummary": "Read data-loader.ts", "outputSummary": "585 lines", "status": "success", "durationEstimate": "fast" },
   102	      { "tool": "Edit", "inputSummary": "Added deprecation warning", "outputSummary": "Inserted 10 lines", "status": "success" }
   103	    ],
   104	    "exchanges": [
   105	      { "userInput": "Implement the JSON-primary plan", "assistantResponse": "Updated 8 files...", "timestamp": "ISO-8601" }
   106	    ],
   107	    "preflight": {
   108	      "knowledgeScore": 40,
   109	      "uncertaintyScore": 60,
   110	      "contextScore": 50,
   111	      "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
   112	      "gaps": ["gap1", "gap2"],
   113	      "confidence": 45,
   114	      "readiness": "Needs research"
   115	    },
   116	    "postflight": {
   117	      "knowledgeScore": 75,
   118	      "uncertaintyScore": 25,
   119	      "contextScore": 80,
   120	      "gapsClosed": ["gap1"],
   121	      "newGaps": ["new-gap"]
   122	    }
   123	  }
   124	
   125	  Tool/Exchange enrichment fields (all optional — JSON-mode only):
   126	  - toolCalls[]: AI-summarized tool calls with tool name, input/output summaries, status, duration
   127	  - exchanges[]: Key user-assistant exchanges with timestamps
   128	  - These provide richer context than DB extraction since the AI filters noise at source
   129	
   130	  Learning Delta Calculation:
   131	  - Knowledge Delta = postflight.knowledgeScore - preflight.knowledgeScore
   132	  - Uncertainty Reduction = preflight.uncertaintyScore - postflight.uncertaintyScore
   133	  - Context Delta = postflight.contextScore - preflight.contextScore
   134	  - Learning Index = (Know x 0.4) + (Uncert x 0.35) + (Context x 0.25)
   135	`;

   140	// 2.1 SIGNAL HANDLERS
   141	let signalHandlersInstalled = false;
   142	let shuttingDown = false;
   143	// Robustness: signal handler releases locks before reporting
   144	function handleSignalShutdown(signal) {
   145	    if (shuttingDown)
   146	        return; // prevent re-entrant handling
   147	    shuttingDown = true;
   148	    let lockReleaseFailed = false;
   149	    try {
   150	        (0, workflow_1.releaseFilesystemLock)();
   151	    }
   152	    catch (_err) {
   153	        lockReleaseFailed = true;
   154	    }
   155	    console.error(`\nWarning: Received ${signal} signal, shutting down gracefully...`);
   156	    process.exit(lockReleaseFailed ? 1 : 0);
   157	}
   158	function installSignalHandlers() {
   159	    if (signalHandlersInstalled) {
   160	        return;
   161	    }
   162	    process.on('SIGTERM', () => handleSignalShutdown('SIGTERM'));
   163	    process.on('SIGINT', () => handleSignalShutdown('SIGINT'));
   164	    signalHandlersInstalled = true;
   165	}

   767	    const snap = getSessionSnapshot();
   768	    const hasData = snap.specFolder || snap.graphFreshness !== 'error' || snap.sessionQuality !== 'unknown';
   769	    if (hasData) {
   770	      const recommended = !snap.primed ? 'call session_bootstrap()' :
   771	        snap.graphFreshness === 'empty' ? 'run code_graph_scan' :
   772	        snap.sessionQuality === 'critical' ? 'call memory_context(resume)' : 'ready';
   773	      lines.push('');
   774	      lines.push('## Session Recovery');
   775	      lines.push(`- Last spec folder: ${snap.specFolder || 'none'}`);
   776	      lines.push(`- Code graph: ${snap.graphFreshness}`);
   777	      lines.push(`- Session quality: ${snap.sessionQuality}`);
   778	      lines.push(`- Recommended: ${recommended}`);
   779	    }
   780	  } catch { /* session-snapshot not available — skip digest */ }
   781	
   782	  // Phase 027: Structural bootstrap guidance for non-hook runtimes
   783	  lines.push('');
   784	  lines.push('## Structural Bootstrap (Phase 027)');
   785	  lines.push('Non-hook runtimes receive automatic structural context via session_bootstrap, session_resume, and auto-prime.');
   786	  lines.push('- If structural context shows "ready": code_graph_query is available for structural lookups');
   787	  lines.push('- If "stale" or "missing": call session_bootstrap first to refresh structural context');
   788	  lines.push('- Recovery priority: session_bootstrap → session_resume → code_graph_scan');
   789	
   790	  // Phase 024: Tool routing decision tree
   791	  try {
   792	    const { getSessionSnapshot: getSnap } = await import('./lib/session/session-snapshot.js');
   793	    const snap = getSnap();
   794	    const routingRules: string[] = [];
   795	    if (snap.cocoIndexAvailable) {
   796	      routingRules.push('Semantic/concept code search → mcp__cocoindex_code__search');
   797	    }
   798	    if (snap.graphFreshness === 'fresh' || snap.graphFreshness === 'stale') {
   799	      routingRules.push('Structural queries (callers, imports, deps) → code_graph_query');
   800	    }
   801	    routingRules.push('Exact text/regex matching → Grep tool');
   802	    if (routingRules.length > 0) {
   803	      lines.push('');
   804	      lines.push('## Tool Routing');
   805	      for (const rule of routingRules) {
   806	        lines.push(`- ${rule}`);
   807	      }
   808	    }
   809	  } catch { /* tool routing snapshot unavailable — skip */ }
   810	
   811	  return lines.filter(Boolean).join(' ');
   812	}

     5	
     6	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     7	// 1. OVERVIEW
     8	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	// Thin OpenCode plugin hook layer for packet 030.
    10	// Important runtime boundary: never import the MCP server bundle directly
    11	// inside the OpenCode host process because its native modules (better-sqlite3,
    12	// sqlite-vec) may be compiled for a different Node ABI than the host runtime.
    13	// Instead, call a plain `node` bridge process that loads the server bundle in
    14	// the user's normal Node environment and returns the transport plan as JSON.

    31	
    32	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    34	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35	
    36	const PLUGIN_ID = 'spec-kit-compact-code-graph';
    37	const DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS = 5000;
    38	const DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;
    39	const DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY = 'node';

    41	const MESSAGES_TRANSFORM_ENABLED = true;
    42	const MESSAGES_TRANSFORM_MODE = 'schema_aligned';
    43	const SYNTHETIC_METADATA_KEY = 'specKitCompactCodeGraph';
    44	const BRIDGE_PATH = fileURLToPath(new URL('./spec-kit-compact-code-graph-bridge.mjs', import.meta.url));
    45	
    46	/**
    47	 * @typedef {{
    48	 *   cacheTtlMs?: number,
    49	 *   specFolder?: string,
    50	 *   nodeBinary?: string,
    51	 *   bridgeTimeoutMs?: number,
    52	 * }} PluginOptions
    53	 */
    54	
    55	/**
    56	 * @typedef {{
    57	 *   hook: string,
    58	 *   title: string,
    59	 *   payloadKind: string,
    60	 *   dedupeKey: string,
    61	 *   content: string,
    62	 * }} TransportBlock
    63	 */
    64	
    65	/**
    66	 * @typedef {{

    88	
    89	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    92	
    93	function normalizePositiveInt(value, fallback) {
    94	  return Number.isFinite(value) && value > 0 ? Math.trunc(value) : fallback;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function normalizeOptions(rawOptions) {
    98	  if (!rawOptions || typeof rawOptions !== 'object') {
    99	    return {
   100	      cacheTtlMs: DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS,
   101	      specFolder: undefined,
   102	      nodeBinary: process.env.SPEC_KIT_PLUGIN_NODE_BINARY || DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY,
   103	      bridgeTimeoutMs: DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
   104	    };
   105	  }
   106	
   107	  const options = /** @type {PluginOptions} */ (rawOptions);
   108	  return {
   109	    cacheTtlMs: normalizePositiveInt(options.cacheTtlMs, DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS),
   110	    specFolder: typeof options.specFolder === 'string' && options.specFolder.trim()
   111	      ? options.specFolder.trim()
   112	      : undefined,
   113	    nodeBinary: typeof options.nodeBinary === 'string' && options.nodeBinary.trim()
   114	      ? options.nodeBinary.trim()
   115	      : (process.env.SPEC_KIT_PLUGIN_NODE_BINARY || DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY),
   116	    bridgeTimeoutMs: normalizePositiveInt(options.bridgeTimeoutMs, DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS),
   117	  };
   118	}
   119	
   120	function cacheKeyForSession(sessionID, specFolder) {
   121	  return `${specFolder ?? '__workspace__'}::${sessionID ?? '__global__'}`;
   122	}
   123	
   124	function parseTransportPlan(responseText) {
   125	  if (!responseText) {
   126	    return null;
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  try {
   130	    const parsed = JSON.parse(responseText);
   131	    const data = parsed?.data ?? parsed;
   132	    const plan = data?.opencodeTransport;
   133	    if (!plan || typeof plan !== 'object') {
   134	      return null;
   135	    }
   136	    if (plan.transportOnly !== true || !Array.isArray(plan.messagesTransform)) {
   137	      return null;
   138	    }
   139	    return /** @type {TransportPlan} */ (plan);
   140	  } catch {
   141	    return null;
   142	  }
   143	}
   144	
   145	function stringifyError(error) {
   146	  if (!error) {
   147	    return 'Unknown bridge error';
   148	  }
   149	  if (error instanceof Error) {
   150	    return error.message;
   151	  }
   152	  return String(error);
   153	}
   154	
   155	function execFilePromise(file, args, options) {
   156	  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   157	    execFile(file, args, options, (error, stdout, stderr) => {
   158	      if (error) {
   159	        reject(error);
   160	        return;
   161	      }
   162	      resolve({
   163	        stdout: typeof stdout === 'string' ? stdout : '',
   164	        stderr: typeof stderr === 'string' ? stderr : '',
   165	      });
   166	    });
   167	  });
   168	}
   169	
   170	async function runTransportBridge({ projectDir, specFolder, nodeBinary, bridgeTimeoutMs }) {
   171	  const args = [BRIDGE_PATH, '--minimal'];
   172	  if (specFolder) {
   173	    args.push('--spec-folder', specFolder);
   174	  }
   175	
   176	  const result = await execFilePromise(nodeBinary, args, {
   177	    cwd: projectDir,
   178	    env: process.env,
   179	    timeout: bridgeTimeoutMs,
   180	    maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024,
   181	  });
   182	  return parseTransportPlan(result.stdout.trim());
   183	}
   184	
   185	async function loadTransportPlan({ projectDir, sessionID, specFolder, cacheTtlMs, nodeBinary, bridgeTimeoutMs }) {
   186	  const key = cacheKeyForSession(sessionID, specFolder);
   187	  const now = Date.now();
   188	  const cached = transportCache.get(key);
   189	
   190	  if (cached && cached.expiresAt > now) {
   191	    return cached.plan;
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  try {
   195	    const plan = await runTransportBridge({
   196	      projectDir,
   197	      specFolder,
   198	      nodeBinary,
   199	      bridgeTimeoutMs,
   200	    });
   201	
   202	    if (!plan) {
   203	      lastRuntimeError = 'Bridge returned no OpenCode transport payload';
   204	      runtimeReady = false;
   205	      transportCache.delete(key);
   206	      return null;
   207	    }
   208	
   209	    runtimeReady = true;
   210	    lastRuntimeError = null;
   211	    transportCache.set(key, {
   212	      plan,
   213	      expiresAt: now + cacheTtlMs,
   214	      updatedAt: new Date(now).toISOString(),
   215	    });
   216	    return plan;
   217	  } catch (error) {
   218	    runtimeReady = false;
   219	    lastRuntimeError = stringifyError(error);
   220	    transportCache.delete(key);
   221	    return null;
   222	  }
   223	}
   224	
   225	function extractEventSessionID(event) {
   226	  if (!event || typeof event !== 'object') {
   227	    return null;
   228	  }
   229	
   230	  if (typeof event.sessionID === 'string') {
   231	    return event.sessionID;
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  if (event.properties && typeof event.properties === 'object') {
   235	    if (typeof event.properties.sessionID === 'string') {
   236	      return event.properties.sessionID;
   237	    }
   238	    if (event.properties.info && typeof event.properties.info === 'object') {
   239	      if (typeof event.properties.info.sessionID === 'string') {
   240	        return event.properties.info.sessionID;

     2	// MODULE: Temporal Edges
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Temporal validity for causal edges
     5	// Adds valid_at / invalid_at lifecycle columns to causal_edges,
     6	// enabling edge invalidation and temporally-scoped graph queries.
     7	// Feature-gated via SPECKIT_TEMPORAL_EDGES (default OFF).
     8	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     9	
    10	import { isTemporalEdgesEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
    11	
    12	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13	// 1. TYPES
    14	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	
    16	export interface Edge {
    17	  sourceId: number;
    18	  targetId: number;
    19	  relation: string;
    20	  strength: number;
    21	  validAt: string | null;
    22	  invalidAt: string | null;
    23	}
    24	
    25	export type TemporalEdge = Edge;
    26	
    27	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	// 2. SCHEMA MIGRATION
    29	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30	
    31	/**
    32	 * Add valid_at and invalid_at columns to causal_edges if not present.
    33	 * Uses ALTER TABLE with try/catch for idempotency — re-running is safe.
    34	 */
    35	export function ensureTemporalColumns(db: Database.Database): void {
    36	  if (!isTemporalEdgesEnabled()) {
    37	    return;
    38	  }
    39	
    40	  try {
    41	    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE causal_edges ADD COLUMN valid_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL`);
    42	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    43	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    44	    if (!/duplicate column name/i.test(message)) {
    45	      console.warn(`[temporal-edges] ensureTemporalColumns(valid_at) failed (fail-open): ${message}`);
    46	    }
    47	  }
    48	
    49	  try {
    50	    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE causal_edges ADD COLUMN invalid_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL`);
    51	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    52	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    53	    if (!/duplicate column name/i.test(message)) {
    54	      console.warn(`[temporal-edges] ensureTemporalColumns(invalid_at) failed (fail-open): ${message}`);
    55	    }
    56	  }
    57	}
    58	
    59	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60	// 3. EDGE INVALIDATION
    61	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Mark an edge as invalidated (set invalid_at to current ISO timestamp).
    65	 * Optionally records the reason in the evidence column.
    66	 * No-op if the edge does not exist or is already invalidated.
    67	 */
    68	export function invalidateEdge(
    69	  db: Database.Database,
    70	  sourceId: number,
    71	  targetId: number,
    72	  reason: string = 'Edge invalidated',
    73	  relation?: string,
    74	): void {
    75	  if (!isTemporalEdgesEnabled()) {
    76	    return;
    77	  }
    78	
    79	  try {
    80	    ensureTemporalColumns(db);
    81	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    82	
    83	    if (relation) {
    84	      (db.prepare(`
    85	        UPDATE causal_edges
    86	        SET invalid_at = ?, evidence = COALESCE(evidence || ' | ', '') || ?
    87	        WHERE source_id = ? AND target_id = ? AND relation = ? AND invalid_at IS NULL
    88	      `) as Database.Statement).run(now, reason, String(sourceId), String(targetId), relation);
    89	      return;
    90	    }
    91	
    92	    (db.prepare(`
    93	      UPDATE causal_edges
    94	      SET invalid_at = ?, evidence = COALESCE(evidence || ' | ', '') || ?
    95	      WHERE source_id = ? AND target_id = ? AND invalid_at IS NULL
    96	    `) as Database.Statement).run(now, reason, String(sourceId), String(targetId));
    97	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    98	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    99	    console.warn(`[temporal-edges] invalidateEdge failed (fail-open): ${message}`);
   100	  }
   101	}
   102	
   103	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   104	// 4. VALID EDGE RETRIEVAL
   105	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   106	
   107	/**
   108	 * Get only currently valid edges for a node (invalid_at IS NULL).
   109	 * Returns edges where the node appears as either source or target.
   110	 */
   111	export function getValidEdges(db: Database.Database, nodeId: number): Edge[] {
   112	  if (!isTemporalEdgesEnabled()) {
   113	    return [];
   114	  }
   115	
   116	  try {
   117	    ensureTemporalColumns(db);
   118	    const rows = (db.prepare(`
   119	      SELECT source_id, target_id, relation, COALESCE(strength, 1.0) AS strength,
   120	             valid_at, invalid_at
   121	      FROM causal_edges
   122	      WHERE (source_id = ? OR target_id = ?) AND invalid_at IS NULL
   123	    `) as Database.Statement).all(String(nodeId), String(nodeId)) as Array<{
   124	      source_id: string;
   125	      target_id: string;
   126	      relation: string;
   127	      strength: number;
   128	      valid_at: string | null;
   129	      invalid_at: string | null;
   130	    }>;
   131	
   132	    return rows.map((row) => ({
   133	      sourceId: Number.parseInt(row.source_id, 10),
   134	      targetId: Number.parseInt(row.target_id, 10),
   135	      relation: row.relation,
   136	      strength: row.strength,
   137	      validAt: row.valid_at,
   138	      invalidAt: row.invalid_at,
   139	    }));
   140	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   141	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   142	    console.warn(`[temporal-edges] getValidEdges failed (fail-open): ${message}`);
   143	    return [];
   144	  }
   145	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
   350	
   351	    const result = db.prepare(`
   352	      UPDATE memory_index
   353	      SET stability = ?,
   354	          updated_at = datetime('now')
   355	      WHERE id = ?
   356	    `).run(clamped_stability, memory_id);
   357	
   358	    return result.changes > 0;
   359	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   360	    console.warn(`[corrections] Could not update stability for memory ${memory_id}: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
   361	    return false;
   362	  }
   363	}
   364	
   365	function map_correction_type_to_relation(correction_type: string): string {
   366	  switch (correction_type) {
   367	    case CORRECTION_TYPES.SUPERSEDED:
   368	    case CORRECTION_TYPES.DEPRECATED:
   369	      return 'supersedes';
   370	    case CORRECTION_TYPES.REFINED:
   371	    case CORRECTION_TYPES.MERGED:
   372	      return 'derived_from';
   373	    default:
   374	      return 'supersedes';
   375	  }
   376	}
   377	
   378	function build_owned_edge_evidence(correction_id: number, correction_type: string, reason: string | null | undefined): string {
   379	  return `Correction#${correction_id}: ${correction_type}${reason ? ' - ' + reason : ''}`;
   380	}
   381	
   382	function build_legacy_edge_evidence(correction_type: string, reason: string | null | undefined): string {
   383	  return `Correction: ${correction_type}${reason ? ' - ' + reason : ''}`;
   384	}
   385	
   386	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   387	   5. CORE CORRECTION FUNCTIONS
   388	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   389	
   390	/**
   391	 * Provides the record_correction helper.
   392	 */
   393	export function record_correction(params: RecordCorrectionParams): CorrectionResult {
   394	  if (!db) {
   395	    throw new Error('[corrections] Database not initialized. Call init() first.');
   396	  }
   397	
   398	  if (!areRelationsEnabled()) {
   399	    return {
   400	      success: false,
   401	      skipped: true,
   402	      reason: 'SPECKIT_RELATIONS disabled (CHK-069)'
   403	    };
   404	  }
   405	
   406	  const {
   407	    original_memory_id,
   408	    correction_memory_id = null,
   409	    correction_type,
   410	    reason = null,
   411	    corrected_by = null
   412	  } = params;
   413	
   414	  // Validate required fields
   415	  if (!original_memory_id) {
   416	    throw new Error('original_memory_id is required');
   417	  }
   418	  if (!correction_type) {
   419	    throw new Error('correction_type is required');
   420	  }
   421	
   422	  // Validate correction type (T054)
   423	  const valid_types = get_correction_types();
   424	  if (!valid_types.includes(correction_type)) {
   425	    throw new Error(`correction_type must be one of: ${valid_types.join(', ')}`);
   426	  }
   427	
   428	  // Prevent self-correction
   429	  if (correction_memory_id && original_memory_id === correction_memory_id) {
   430	    throw new Error('original_memory_id and correction_memory_id cannot be the same');
   431	  }
   432	
   433	  // Get current stability values
   434	  const original_stability_before = get_memory_stability(original_memory_id);
   435	  if (original_stability_before === null) {
   436	    throw new Error(`Original memory ${original_memory_id} not found`);
   437	  }
   438	
   439	  let correction_stability_before: number | null = null;
   440	  if (correction_memory_id) {
   441	    correction_stability_before = get_memory_stability(correction_memory_id);
   442	    if (correction_stability_before === null) {
   443	      throw new Error(`Correction memory ${correction_memory_id} not found`);
   444	    }
   445	  }
   446	
   447	  // Use transaction for atomicity
   448	  const run_correction = db.transaction(() => {
   449	    // Apply stability penalty to original memory (T053: 0.5x penalty)
   450	    const original_stability_after = original_stability_before * CORRECTION_STABILITY_PENALTY;
   451	    set_memory_stability(original_memory_id, original_stability_after);
   452	
   453	    // Apply stability boost to correction memory if exists (T055: 1.2x boost)
   454	    let correction_stability_after: number | null = null;
   455	    if (correction_memory_id && correction_stability_before !== null) {
   456	      correction_stability_after = correction_stability_before * REPLACEMENT_STABILITY_BOOST;
   457	      set_memory_stability(correction_memory_id, correction_stability_after);
   458	    }
   459	
   460	    // Record the correction
   461	    // Record_correction validates db before starting this transaction
   462	    const stmt = db!.prepare(`
   463	      INSERT INTO memory_corrections (
   464	        original_memory_id,
   465	        correction_memory_id,
   466	        correction_type,
   467	        original_stability_before,
   468	        original_stability_after,
   469	        correction_stability_before,
   470	        correction_stability_after,
   471	        reason,
   472	        corrected_by,
   473	        created_at
   474	      ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
   475	    `);
   476	
   477	    const result = stmt.run(
   478	      original_memory_id,
   479	      correction_memory_id,
   480	      correction_type,
   481	      original_stability_before,
   482	      original_stability_after,
   483	      correction_stability_before,
   484	      correction_stability_after,
   485	      reason,
   486	      corrected_by
   487	    );
   488	
   489	    // Also create a causal edge if causal_edges table exists
   490	    try {
   491	      // Record_correction validates db before starting this transaction
   492	      const causal_table_exists = db!.prepare(`
   493	        SELECT name FROM sqlite_master
   494	        WHERE type='table' AND name='causal_edges'
   495	      `).get();
   496	
   497	      if (causal_table_exists && correction_memory_id) {
   498	        const relation = map_correction_type_to_relation(correction_type);
   499	        const correctionId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
   500	        const edgeEvidence = build_owned_edge_evidence(correctionId, correction_type, reason);
   501	
   502	        // Record_correction validates db before starting this transaction
   503	        // Undo_correction throws when db is not initialized before creating this transaction
   504	    db!.prepare(`
   505	          INSERT OR IGNORE INTO causal_edges (
   506	            source_id, target_id, relation, strength, evidence, extracted_at
   507	          ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1.0, ?, datetime('now'))
   508	        `).run(
   509	          String(correction_memory_id),
   510	          String(original_memory_id),
   511	          relation,
   512	          edgeEvidence
   513	        );
   514	        invalidateGraphCaches(db!);
   515	      }
   516	    } catch (e: unknown) {
   517	      // Causal edge creation is optional, don't fail if it errors
   518	      console.warn(`[corrections] Could not create causal edge: ${get_error_message(e)}`);
   519	    }
   520	
   521	    return {
   522	      correction_id: result.lastInsertRowid,
   523	      original_memory_id,
   524	      correction_memory_id,
   525	      correction_type,
   526	      stability_changes: {
   527	        original: {
   528	          before: original_stability_before,
   529	          after: original_stability_after,
   530	          penalty_applied: CORRECTION_STABILITY_PENALTY
   531	        },
   532	        correction: correction_memory_id ? {
   533	          before: correction_stability_before as number,
   534	          after: correction_stability_after as number,
   535	          boost_applied: REPLACEMENT_STABILITY_BOOST
   536	        } : null
   537	      }
   538	    };
   539	  });
   540	
   541	  try {
   542	    const result = run_correction();
   543	    return {
   544	      success: true,
   545	      ...result
   546	    };
   547	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   548	    console.error(`[corrections] record_correction failed: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
   549	    throw error;
   550	  }
   551	}
   552	
   553	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   554	   6. UNDO CAPABILITY
   555	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   556	
   557	/**
   558	 * Provides the undo_correction helper.
   559	 */
   560	export function undo_correction(correction_id: number): UndoResult {
   600	      );
   601	    }
   602	
   603	    // Mark correction as undone
   604	    db!.prepare(`
   605	      UPDATE memory_corrections
   606	      SET is_undone = 1,
   607	          undone_at = datetime('now')
   608	      WHERE id = ?
   609	    `).run(correction_id);
   610	
   611	    // Try to remove the causal edge if it exists
   612	    // T-02: Scope deletion by relation type to avoid removing unrelated edges
   613	    // Between the same pair of memories (e.g., 'supersedes' vs 'derived_from').
   614	    try {
   615	      if (correction.correction_memory_id) {
   616	        const undoRelation = map_correction_type_to_relation(correction.correction_type);
   617	        const ownedEdgeEvidencePrefix = `Correction#${correction_id}:`;
   618	
   619	        // Undo_correction throws when db is not initialized before creating this transaction
   620	        const deleteResult = db!.prepare(`
   621	          DELETE FROM causal_edges
   622	          WHERE source_id = ?
   623	            AND target_id = ?
   624	            AND relation = ?
   625	            AND evidence LIKE ?
   626	        `).run(
   627	          String(correction.correction_memory_id),
   628	          String(correction.original_memory_id),
   629	          undoRelation,
   630	          `${ownedEdgeEvidencePrefix}%`
   631	        );
   632	        invalidateGraphCaches(db!);
   633	
   634	        if ((deleteResult as { changes: number }).changes === 0) {
   635	          const legacyEdgeEvidence = build_legacy_edge_evidence(correction.correction_type, correction.reason);
   636	
   637	          const legacyDeleteResult = db!.prepare(`
   638	            DELETE FROM causal_edges
   639	            WHERE source_id = ?
   640	              AND target_id = ?
   641	              AND relation = ?
   642	              AND evidence = ?
   643	          `).run(
   644	            String(correction.correction_memory_id),
   645	            String(correction.original_memory_id),
   646	            undoRelation,
   647	            legacyEdgeEvidence
   648	          );
   649	          invalidateGraphCaches(db!);
   650	
   651	          if ((legacyDeleteResult as { changes: number }).changes === 0) {
   652	            console.warn(`[corrections] undo: no causal edge found for (${correction.correction_memory_id} → ${correction.original_memory_id}, relation=${undoRelation})`);
   653	          }
   654	        }
   655	      }
   656	    } catch (edgeErr: unknown) {
   657	      // T-02: Surface edge-deletion errors instead of empty catch
   658	      console.warn(`[corrections] undo: causal edge deletion failed: ${get_error_message(edgeErr)}`);
   659	    }
   660	
   661	    return {
   662	      correction_id,
   663	      original_memory_id: correction.original_memory_id,
   664	      correction_memory_id: correction.correction_memory_id,
   665	      stability_restored: {
   666	        original: correction.original_stability_before,
   667	        correction: correction.correction_stability_before
   668	      }
   669	    };
   670	  });

     1	"""
     2	test_knowledge_graph.py — Tests for the temporal knowledge graph.
     3	
     4	Covers: entity CRUD, triple CRUD, temporal queries, invalidation,
     5	timeline, stats, and edge cases (duplicate triples, ID collisions).
     6	"""
     7	
     8	
     9	class TestEntityOperations:
    10	    def test_add_entity(self, kg):
    11	        eid = kg.add_entity("Alice", entity_type="person")
    12	        assert eid == "alice"
    13	
    14	    def test_add_entity_normalizes_id(self, kg):
    15	        eid = kg.add_entity("Dr. Chen", entity_type="person")
    16	        assert eid == "dr._chen"
    17	
    18	    def test_add_entity_upsert(self, kg):
    19	        kg.add_entity("Alice", entity_type="person")
    20	        kg.add_entity("Alice", entity_type="engineer")
    21	        # Should not raise — INSERT OR REPLACE
    22	        stats = kg.stats()
    23	        assert stats["entities"] == 1
    24	
    25	
    26	class TestTripleOperations:
    27	    def test_add_triple_creates_entities(self, kg):
    28	        tid = kg.add_triple("Alice", "knows", "Bob")
    29	        assert tid.startswith("t_alice_knows_bob_")
    30	        stats = kg.stats()
    31	        assert stats["entities"] == 2  # auto-created
    32	
    33	    def test_add_triple_with_dates(self, kg):
    34	        tid = kg.add_triple("Max", "does", "swimming", valid_from="2025-01-01")
    35	        assert tid.startswith("t_max_does_swimming_")
    36	
    37	    def test_duplicate_triple_returns_existing_id(self, kg):
    38	        tid1 = kg.add_triple("Alice", "knows", "Bob")
    39	        tid2 = kg.add_triple("Alice", "knows", "Bob")
    40	        assert tid1 == tid2
    41	
    42	    def test_invalidated_triple_allows_re_add(self, kg):
    43	        tid1 = kg.add_triple("Alice", "works_at", "Acme")
    44	        kg.invalidate("Alice", "works_at", "Acme", ended="2025-01-01")
    45	        tid2 = kg.add_triple("Alice", "works_at", "Acme")
    46	        assert tid1 != tid2  # new triple since old one was closed
    47	
    48	
    49	class TestQueries:
    50	    def test_query_outgoing(self, seeded_kg):
    51	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Alice", direction="outgoing")
    52	        predicates = {r["predicate"] for r in results}
    53	        assert "parent_of" in predicates
    54	        assert "works_at" in predicates
    55	
    56	    def test_query_incoming(self, seeded_kg):
    57	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Max", direction="incoming")
    58	        assert any(r["subject"] == "Alice" and r["predicate"] == "parent_of" for r in results)
    59	
    60	    def test_query_both_directions(self, seeded_kg):
    61	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Max", direction="both")
    62	        directions = {r["direction"] for r in results}
    63	        assert "outgoing" in directions
    64	        assert "incoming" in directions
    65	
    66	    def test_query_as_of_filters_expired(self, seeded_kg):
    67	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Alice", as_of="2023-06-01", direction="outgoing")
    68	        employers = [r["object"] for r in results if r["predicate"] == "works_at"]
    69	        assert "Acme Corp" in employers
    70	        assert "NewCo" not in employers
    71	
    72	    def test_query_as_of_shows_current(self, seeded_kg):
    73	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Alice", as_of="2025-06-01", direction="outgoing")
    74	        employers = [r["object"] for r in results if r["predicate"] == "works_at"]
    75	        assert "NewCo" in employers
    76	        assert "Acme Corp" not in employers
    77	
    78	    def test_query_relationship(self, seeded_kg):
    79	        results = seeded_kg.query_relationship("does")
    80	        assert len(results) == 2  # swimming + chess
    81	
    82	
    83	class TestInvalidation:
    84	    def test_invalidate_sets_valid_to(self, seeded_kg):
    85	        seeded_kg.invalidate("Max", "does", "chess", ended="2026-01-01")
    86	        results = seeded_kg.query_entity("Max", direction="outgoing")
    87	        chess = [r for r in results if r["object"] == "chess"]
    88	        assert len(chess) == 1
    89	        assert chess[0]["valid_to"] == "2026-01-01"
    90	        assert chess[0]["current"] is False
    91	
    92	
    93	class TestTimeline:
    94	    def test_timeline_all(self, seeded_kg):
    95	        tl = seeded_kg.timeline()
    96	        assert len(tl) >= 4
    97	
    98	    def test_timeline_entity(self, seeded_kg):
    99	        tl = seeded_kg.timeline("Max")
   100	        subjects_and_objects = {t["subject"] for t in tl} | {t["object"] for t in tl}
   101	        assert "Max" in subjects_and_objects
   102	
   103	    def test_timeline_global_has_limit(self, kg):
   104	        # Add > 100 triples
   105	        for i in range(105):
   106	            kg.add_triple(f"entity_{i}", "relates_to", f"entity_{i + 1}")
   107	        tl = kg.timeline()
   108	        assert len(tl) == 100  # LIMIT 100
   109	
   110	    def test_timeline_entity_has_limit(self, kg):
   111	        # Add > 100 triples all connected to a single entity
   112	        for i in range(105):
   113	            kg.add_triple(
   114	                "hub", "connects_to", f"spoke_{i}", valid_from=f"2025-01-{(i % 28) + 1:02d}"
   115	            )
   116	        tl = kg.timeline("hub")
   117	        assert len(tl) == 100  # LIMIT 100 on entity-filtered branch
   118	
   119	
   120	class TestWALMode:
   121	    def test_wal_mode_enabled(self, kg):
   122	        conn = kg._conn()
   123	        mode = conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode").fetchone()[0]
   124	        conn.close()
   125	        assert mode == "wal"

   127

   470	    """Knowledge graph overview: entities, triples, relationship types."""
   471	    return _kg.stats()
   472	
   473	
   474	# ==================== AGENT DIARY ====================
   475	
   476	
   477	def tool_diary_write(agent_name: str, entry: str, topic: str = "general"):
   478	    """
   479	    Write a diary entry for this agent. Each agent gets its own wing
   480	    with a diary room. Entries are timestamped and accumulate over time.
   481	
   482	    This is the agent's personal journal — observations, thoughts,
   483	    what it worked on, what it noticed, what it thinks matters.
   484	    """
   485	    try:
   486	        agent_name = sanitize_name(agent_name, "agent_name")
   487	        entry = sanitize_content(entry)
   488	    except ValueError as e:
   489	        return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
   490	
   491	    wing = f"wing_{agent_name.lower().replace(' ', '_')}"
   492	    room = "diary"
   493	    col = _get_collection(create=True)
   494	    if not col:
   495	        return _no_palace()
   496	
   497	    now = datetime.now()
   498	    entry_id = f"diary_{wing}_{now.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}_{hashlib.sha256(entry[:50].encode()).hexdigest()[:12]}"
   499	
   500	    _wal_log(
   501	        "diary_write",
   502	        {
   503	            "agent_name": agent_name,
   504	            "topic": topic,
   505	            "entry_id": entry_id,
   506	            "entry_preview": entry[:200],
   507	        },
   508	    )
   509	
   510	    try:
   511	        # TODO: Future versions should expand AAAK before embedding to improve
   512	        # semantic search quality. For now, store raw AAAK in metadata so it's
   513	        # preserved, and keep the document as-is for embedding (even though
   514	        # compressed AAAK degrades embedding quality).
   515	        col.add(
   516	            ids=[entry_id],
   517	            documents=[entry],
   518	            metadatas=[
   519	                {
   520	                    "wing": wing,
   521	                    "room": room,
   522	                    "hall": "hall_diary",
   523	                    "topic": topic,
   524	                    "type": "diary_entry",
   525	                    "agent": agent_name,
   526	                    "filed_at": now.isoformat(),
   527	                    "date": now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
   528	                }
   529	            ],
   530	        )
   531	        logger.info(f"Diary entry: {entry_id} → {wing}/diary/{topic}")
   532	        return {
   533	            "success": True,
   534	            "entry_id": entry_id,
   535	            "agent": agent_name,
   536	            "topic": topic,
   537	            "timestamp": now.isoformat(),
   538	        }
   539	    except Exception as e:
   540	        return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
   541	
   542	
   543	def tool_diary_read(agent_name: str, last_n: int = 10):
   544	    """
   545	    Read an agent's recent diary entries. Returns the last N entries
   546	    in chronological order — the agent's personal journal.
   547	    """
   548	    wing = f"wing_{agent_name.lower().replace(' ', '_')}"
   549	    col = _get_collection()
   550	    if not col:
   551	        return _no_palace()
   552	
   553	    try:
   554	        results = col.get(
   555	            where={"$and": [{"wing": wing}, {"room": "diary"}]},
   556	            include=["documents", "metadatas"],
   557	            limit=10000,
   558	        )
   559	
   560	        if not results["ids"]:
   561	            return {"agent": agent_name, "entries": [], "message": "No diary entries yet."}
   562	
   563	        # Combine and sort by timestamp
   564	        entries = []
   565	        for doc, meta in zip(results["documents"], results["metadatas"]):
   566	            entries.append(
   567	                {
   568	                    "date": meta.get("date", ""),
   569	                    "timestamp": meta.get("filed_at", ""),
   570	                    "topic": meta.get("topic", ""),
   571	                    "content": doc,
   572	                }
   573	            )
   574	
   575	        entries.sort(key=lambda x: x["timestamp"], reverse=True)
   576	        entries = entries[:last_n]
   577	
   578	        return {
   579	            "agent": agent_name,
   580	            "entries": entries,
   581	            "total": len(results["ids"]),
   582	            "showing": len(entries),
   583	        }
   584	    except Exception as e:
   585	        return {"error": str(e)}
   586	
   587	
   588	# ==================== MCP PROTOCOL ====================
   589	
   590	TOOLS = {
   591	    "mempalace_status": {
   592	        "description": "Palace overview — total drawers, wing and room counts",
   593	        "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
   594	        "handler": tool_status,
   595	    },
   596	    "mempalace_list_wings": {
   597	        "description": "List all wings with drawer counts",
   598	        "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
   599	        "handler": tool_list_wings,
   600	    },
   601	    "mempalace_list_rooms": {
   602	        "description": "List rooms within a wing (or all rooms if no wing given)",
   603	        "input_schema": {
   604	            "type": "object",
   605	            "properties": {
   606	                "wing": {"type": "string", "description": "Wing to list rooms for (optional)"},
   607	            },
   608	        },
   609	        "handler": tool_list_rooms,
   610	    },
   611	    "mempalace_get_taxonomy": {
   612	        "description": "Full taxonomy: wing → room → drawer count",
   613	        "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
   614	        "handler": tool_get_taxonomy,
   615	    },
   616	    "mempalace_get_aaak_spec": {
   617	        "description": "Get the AAAK dialect specification — the compressed memory format MemPalace uses. Call this if you need to read or write AAAK-compressed memories.",
   618	        "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
   619	        "handler": tool_get_aaak_spec,
   620	    },

/bin/zsh -lc 'cd /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public && rg -n "knowledge graph|temporal fact|timeline|validity|fact plane|causal graph" .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md' in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
301:**Intent classification** maps your query to one of 7 task types (`add_feature`, `fix_bug`, `refactor`, `security_audit`, `understand`, `find_spec`, `find_decision`). Each type has its own channel weight profile. A `find_decision` query boosts the causal graph channel. A `fix_bug` query boosts exact-match channels.
463:**Two-tier explainability** -- basic mode shows a plain-language reason ("matched strongly on meaning, boosted by causal graph connection"). Debug mode shows exact channel contributions and weights.
1012:Get statistics about the causal graph: total edges, coverage percentage and breakdown by relationship type. Target coverage is 60% of memories linked.
1687:Three main differences. First, this system uses 5 search channels combined with rank fusion, beyond vector similarity. Second, it applies FSRS decay so recently accessed memories rank higher without manual curation. Third, the causal graph lets you answer "why was this decision made?" which no vector database supports natively.

/bin/zsh -lc 'cd /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public && rg -n "April 7|correction|knowledge graph|temporal|timeline|invalidate|How You Actually Use It|status|kg_query|kg_invalidate|diary" .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md' in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
19:**AAAK (experimental)** — A lossy abbreviation dialect for packing repeated entities into fewer tokens at scale. Readable by any LLM that reads text — Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama, Mistral — no decoder needed. **AAAK is a separate compression layer, not the storage default**, and on the LongMemEval benchmark it currently regresses vs raw mode (84.2% vs 96.6%). We're iterating. See the [note above](#a-note-from-milla--ben--april-7-2026) for the honest status.
52:## A Note from Milla & Ben — April 7, 2026
64:> - **"Contradiction detection"** exists as a separate utility (`fact_checker.py`) but is not currently wired into the knowledge graph operations as the README implied.
104:mempalace status
111:## How You Actually Use It
290:**Honest status (April 2026):**
302:A separate utility (`fact_checker.py`) can check assertions against entity facts. It's not currently called automatically by the knowledge graph operations — this is being fixed (track in [Issue #27](https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues/27)). When enabled it catches things like:
315:Facts checked against the knowledge graph. Ages, dates, and tenures calculated dynamically — not hardcoded.
389:kg.timeline("Orion")
393:Facts have validity windows. When something stops being true, invalidate it:
396:kg.invalidate("Kai", "works_on", "Orion", ended="2026-03-01")
413:Create agents that focus on specific areas. Each agent gets its own wing and diary in the palace — not in your CLAUDE.md. Add 50 agents, your config stays the same size.
431:- **Keeps a diary** — written in AAAK, persists across sessions
435:# Agent writes to its diary after a code review
436:mempalace_diary_write("reviewer",
440:mempalace_diary_read("reviewer", last_n=10)
467:| `mempalace_status` | Palace overview + AAAK spec + memory protocol |
486:| `mempalace_kg_query` | Entity relationships with time filtering |
488:| `mempalace_kg_invalidate` | Mark facts as ended |
489:| `mempalace_kg_timeline` | Chronological entity story |
504:| `mempalace_diary_write` | Write AAAK diary entry |
505:| `mempalace_diary_read` | Read recent diary entries |
507:The AI learns AAAK and the memory protocol automatically from the `mempalace_status` response. No manual configuration.
587:mempalace status                                  # palace overview
661:│   ├── knowledge_graph.py     ← temporal entity graph

 succeeded in 0ms:
    40	<td align="center"><strong>96.6%</strong><br><sub>LongMemEval R@5<br><b>raw mode</b>, zero API calls</sub></td>
